commit.message:
[Repo Assist] perf: pre-compute navigation menu structure once per build (O(n²) → O(n)) (#1129)

* perf: pre-compute navigation structure once per build (O(n²) → O(n))

Replace GetNavigationEntries (called once per page) with
GetNavigationEntriesFactory, which pre-computes the expensive
filter/group/sort structure once and returns a cheap closure that
only applies IsActive flags and generates HTML.

For a site with n pages, this reduces:
- sorting/grouping: from O(n × n log n) to O(n log n)
- File.Exists calls for template detection: from 2n to 2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* fix: use valid Keep a Changelog subsection type 'Changed' instead of 'Performance'

'### Performance' is not a recognised Keep a Changelog subsection.
Ionide.KeepAChangelog.Tasks 0.3.3 enforces the standard categories
(Added, Changed, Deprecated, Fixed, Removed, Security), causing the
build to fail with IKC0002. Rename to '### Changed'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

* tests: add unit tests for GetNavigationEntriesFactory (PR #1129)

15 tests covering:
- Empty input → empty output
- Single uncategorized model → Documentation header
- None currentPagePath → no active item
- Matching/non-matching page path → correct active state
- Exactly one active item among multiple pages
- Exclusion of isOtherLang, non-HTML, and index file models
- ignoreUncategorized flag (true and false)
- Category ordering by CategoryIndex
- Item ordering within a category by Index
- Factory idempotency (multiple calls, same result)
- Per-page active state correctness across successive calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add baseline support for Shelly BLU Distance across the BLE gateway path. The app now recognizes the model code and inferred BLE model ID, the BLE helper exposes the BTHome 0x40 measurement as a public distanceMm field, and the event router treats distance readings as continuous child events.

- UniversalDrivers/ShellyBluDistance.groovy: add a dedicated BLU Distance driver exposing distanceMm, battery, presence, and lastUpdated for BLE gateway relays.
- docs/device-coverage-todo.md: remove BLU Distance from the confirmed gap list and refine remaining validation notes.

feat: extend Wall Display support for X2i and XL

Add model routing in ShellyDeviceManager so the Wall Display X2i and Wall Display XL reuse the Wall Display parent driver instead of depending on the legacy model mapping.

Expand the Wall Display parent driver to handle multi-switch devices, create switch child devices when needed, track parent and child switch state from webhook and status payloads, and mark temperature and humidity as unavailable or not present when newer hardware omits those sensors.

Refresh the coverage TODO document to remove X2i and XL from the confirmed gap list, record the new support path, and leave only remaining validation work for real payload capture and XL button-event confirmation.

- Apps/ShellyDeviceManager.groovy: add model identifiers and driver mappings for Wall Display X2i and Wall Display XL so discovery routes them to the shared Wall Display parent.
- UniversalDrivers/ShellyWallDisplayParent.groovy: generalize the parent for Wall Display-family devices with multi-switch child support, improved switch state reconciliation, and sensor availability handling for sensor-optional models.
- docs/device-coverage-todo.md: update coverage notes to reflect the new Wall Display support and narrow the remaining follow-up validation.

commit.message:
[http-server-csharp] Emit C# class for models extending another model with no additional properties (#10175)

`isEmptyResponseModel` treats any model with zero own properties as
empty, skipping emission entirely. This means `model Baz extends Foo {}`
never produces a `Baz.cs` file, even though it's a meaningful derived
type users may want to extend via partial classes.

```typespec
model Foo { id: int32; name: string; }
model Baz extends Foo {}  // No Baz.cs emitted
```

### Changes

- **`packages/http-server-csharp/src/lib/utils.ts`**:
`isEmptyResponseModel` now checks `!model.baseModel` before treating
zero-property models as empty — derived models are always emitted.
- **`packages/http-server-csharp/test/generation.test.ts`**: Added test
case verifying a model extending another with no additional properties
produces a class file with correct inheritance.

* Fix backfill overwriting copilotAuthored and restore 208 PRs

During backfill (workflow_dispatch with month_year), the skip logic was
bypassed so all PRs were re-evaluated. PRs detected as Copilot only via
Co-authored-by commit trailers would get copilotAuthored=false initially,
then the 200-PR commit-check limit prevented re-checking them all.

Fix: preserve existing copilotAuthored=true — once a PR is detected as
Copilot, never downgrade it during re-evaluation. This also reduces
unnecessary commit-message API calls during backfills.

commit.message:
Add Pratt/TDOP operator precedence to BNF parser generator

Implement Top-Down Operator Precedence (Pratt) parsing as a first-class
output mode in the examples/bnf parser generator tool.

## What changed

### tableparser.h
- Add PrattParser<ValueType> template base class with:
  - m_infixBP map keyed by token, holding {lbp, rbp} binding powers
  - advance() / matchToken() helpers
  - parseExpr(minBP) — the core Pratt loop
  - Pure virtual initPrattTable(), nud(), led()
  - Default yyparse() that calls parseExpr(0)
- Add <string> and <cstdio> includes

### tableparser.cpp
- Fix vsprintf_s (Windows-only) with a platform-guarded vsnprintf fallback

### bnflexer.h
- Add TV_LEFT, TV_RIGHT, TV_NONASSOC token enum values

### bnfparser.h / bnfparser.cpp
- Add OperatorDecl struct { level, rightAssoc, nonAssoc }
- Parse %left / %right / %nonassoc directives in DoTokens()
  - Operators accumulate in m_operatorDecls with auto-incrementing levels
  - Character terminals ('+'  etc.) and named terminals both supported
- Add DetectExprNonterminals(): marks nonterminals that have infix
  productions of the form  X → X op X
- isPrattMode() returns true when any operator decls are present
- OutputSymbols() branches: PrattParser<YYSTYPE> header vs TableParser
- Add OutputPrattTable() generating:
    initPrattTable()  lbp/rbp from %left/%right declarations
    nud()             atom and grouping cases from non-infix productions
    led()             binary operator cases from infix productions
    yyparse()         advance + parseExpr + start-rule action
- yyparse() skips GenerateTable()/OutputTable() in Pratt mode

### calc.y
- Replace the manual expr/term/factor hierarchy and $< accumulator hack
  with a flat grammar and explicit %left declarations:

    %left  '+' '-'
    %left  '*' '/'

    expr: NUMBER
        | '(' expr ')'  { $$ = $2; }
        | expr '+' expr { $$ = $1 + $3; }
        ...

### README.md
- Document both LL(1) mode and Pratt mode, explain binding powers,
  %left/%right/%nonassoc syntax, and how the generated algorithm works

## Binding power assignment
- %left  op at level L  → {lbp=L, rbp=L}   (left-to-right grouping)
- %right op at level L  → {lbp=L, rbp=L-1} (right-to-left grouping)
- Precedence levels auto-increment per directive line (first line = 1)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
